target: Did the reviewer/steps actually exercise the load-bearing property, or is 'passed' an artifact of a weak/self review? Check the review's independence and coverage.
situation: An agent's plan passed the reviewer and all steps executed. Ship the output? What to check?
axes: Verify output schema compliance against API contract
Check for hallucinated or fabricated data in final payload
Confirm execution logs match the approved plan steps

target: Is the 73% dominance a composition/construction artifact (subset mixing, contamination)? Re-measure under a purified construction before treating it as structure.
situation: A run shows axis contribution is 73% phase+r dominant. Propose what to check before treating it as the system's structure.
axes: Measure the proportion of links that fail to reach the valid subset to detect contamination
Verify if the 73% dominance is an artifact by re-measuring with fresh initialization
Check if the apparent dominant phase is actually a size category artifact

target: Did the reviewer/steps actually exercise the load-bearing property, or is 'passed' an artifact of a weak/self review? Check the review's independence and coverage.
situation: An agent's plan passed the reviewer and all steps executed. Ship the output? What to check?
axes: Verify read triggers are event-driven rather than fixed-interval timers
Audit for hidden researcher-controlled state injection in the read pipeline
Confirm output generation is decoupled from the agent's internal planning loop

target: Is 99.9% an artifact of what the monitor counts (transport success != content/task success)? Confirm the metric definition before treating it as reliability.
situation: A service shows 99.9% success in monitoring. Propose what to check before claiming it is reliable.
axes: Verify if the 99.9% metric excludes critical user-facing errors or only technical HTTP status codes
Audit the monitoring coverage to ensure all entry points, dependencies, and edge cases are instrumented
Analyze error distribution to identify if failures are concentrated in specific regions, times, or user segments

target: Is the 3x an artifact of the benchmark setup (warmup, batch, cache, fixed input)? Vary the setup and measure under real conditions.
situation: A benchmark shows our new kernel is 3x faster. Propose what to check before reporting the speedup.
axes: Verify benchmark uses event-driven reads to prevent fixed-interval timing artifacts
Confirm state buffer synchronization is not introducing hidden latency or blocking
Check for compiler optimizations or warm-up effects skewing the 3x speedup metric

target: The co-serve impossibility is a DECLARED assertion, not measured — measure it first; and the mode choice depends on an unmeasured variable (swap frequency).
situation: Two models are said to be un-co-servable on our GPUs so we must pick an operating mode. What to check before choosing?
axes: GPU memory footprint per model
Inference latency per batch
Throughput under concurrent load

target: Is the 50% saving representative or an artifact of the one trial's conditions (rework frequency, task mix)? Measure across varied conditions before adopting.
situation: Batching cut our run time by half in one trial. Propose what to check before adopting batching.
axes: Verify output correctness against non-batched baseline
Measure GPU memory utilization and peak usage
Assess latency impact of batch serialization overhead

target: Is 'zero issues' grounded, or an artifact of the audit not covering the real failure modes? Attack the audit's coverage before trusting the null.
situation: An automated audit of our pipeline found zero issues. Propose what to check before trusting it.
axes: Verify test coverage completeness and edge case inclusion
Audit the audit tool's configuration and rule set for false negatives
Check for hardcoded credentials or secrets in the pipeline environment

target: Is the jump a property of the mechanism or an artifact of run length/accumulation? Confirm the generating condition before treating the metric as a finding.
situation: A metric jumped from 25% to 77% between short and long runs. Propose what to check before treating 77% as a property.
axes: Check if the 77% value correlates with the number of retired/stale elements accumulated over the run length
Verify if the metric stabilizes or plateaus in extended runs, indicating an accumulation effect rather than a steady-state property
Compare the metric distribution in short runs (25%) against the expected accumulation rate to confirm the construction hypothesis

target: Does the SPECIFIED capability actually work/perform in OUR local config? Docs give no local numbers; measure locally (spec != local applicability).
situation: Vendor docs document a capability with 'fast resume'. We want it to cut model-switch cost. What to check before adopting?
axes: Verify cold-start latency vs. warm-resume latency metrics under production load
Measure context window overhead and token consumption during state restoration
Audit data consistency and model state integrity after resume events

target: Is each finding substantiated by a reproducing counterexample, or an over-flag? Disposition before rework; reject findings that don't reproduce.
situation: A code auditor returned several findings on a validator that passed all its load-bearing tests. Rework based on them? What to check?
axes: Verify static analysis false positives against runtime behavior
Audit edge-case handling in untested input scenarios
Review security implications of unused or dead code paths

target: Are the clusters an artifact of the algorithm/parameter choice (k, distance, seed)? Vary the construction and check stability before treating clusters as real structure.
situation: A clustering run produced 5 clean clusters. Propose what to check before treating them as real groups.
axes: Stability under perturbation (e.g., bootstrap resampling)
Statistical significance of cluster separation (e.g., silhouette score)
Interpretability and domain validity of cluster centroids

target: Does the SPECIFIED capability actually work/perform in OUR local config? Docs give no local numbers; measure locally (spec != local applicability).
situation: Vendor docs document a capability with 'fast resume'. We want it to cut model-switch cost. What to check before adopting?
axes: Measure end-to-end latency variance across different model sizes and hardware configurations
Audit the 'fast resume' implementation to distinguish between cold-start optimization and actual state-transfer overhead
Verify if the resume mechanism preserves model accuracy/precision under load compared to full initialization

target: Is each finding substantiated by a reproducing counterexample, or an over-flag? Disposition before rework; reject findings that don't reproduce.
situation: A code auditor returned several findings on a validator that passed all its load-bearing tests. Rework based on them? What to check?
axes: audit link reachability to valid subset
measure group contamination by invalid subsets

target: Is the 40% an artifact of the experimental setup (sample ratio mismatch, novelty/seasonality, selection)? Confirm the mechanism before treating the number as the effect.
situation: An A/B test shows a striking 40% lift in the treatment. Propose what to check before shipping.
axes: Verify statistical significance and confidence intervals
Check for selection bias or data leakage in the split
